key_dungeons.name: Dragon's Lair
key_dungeons.description: A shattered keep on the far northeastern isle. The cliffs smoke, the surf steams where it strikes the shore — something ancient sleeps below.
key_dungeons.quest_objective: Descend the Dragon's Lair, slay the wyrm, and return with the Dragonheart
key_dungeons.quest_hint: The Sage in Larian said the Sun Sword is the only blade worthy of the task.

game_log: Old Dunmire: I was born in Larian, and so was my father, and his father before him. And none of us — not one — ever saw the sea smoke like it does now. Something wakes, traveler. Something old.
Old Dunmire: The shrine has stood a hundred years. It'll stand a hundred more, gods willing. But only if somebody puts the wyrm back to sleep — or ends it outright.
Phriestess: Hail thee, if thou are in need of healing, walk up to one of our many shrines in our temple.

Meg of the Harborside: Aye, I've sailed as far as the Sun Citadel and back. Cold place, that. But beyond it — northeast, past the wyvern reef — there's an isle that smokes even in winter. No gull lands there. No sane sailor either.
Meg of the Harborside: If thou means to go, bring more than a boat. Bring fire, and a blade that answers to the sun, and a will to come back.

Hollis the Shopkeep: Hail, traveler. Thou hast the look of one who can handle trouble, and I am up to my ears in it. I own the General Shop here in Larian. Some months back I let a goblin lodge in my cellar in exchange for a small favor — the details are not important now. He has since let the place fall to ruin. Rats have overrun the basement, and the goblin himself grows bolder by the day. Clear them all out and I shall see thee well rewarded.
Hollis the Shopkeep: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck out there!
Quest 'Rat Infestation': Clear rats from the General Shop - Complete!
Quest 'Rat Infestation': Evict the goblin squatter - Complete!
Quest 'Rat Infestation': Hunt the deeper nests in the basement - Complete! | All steps done! Return to the quest giver for your reward.
Hollis the Shopkeep: Thank you, hero! Here is your reward: +100 XP, +100 Gold, +2 items (Camping Supplies, Arrows)

Huntsman Puck: The realm is overrun with giant wolves, and no natural pack moves like these do. An orc hand guides them, I'd wager my last arrow on it. Thin the packs first — then find the brute pulling their strings. Do us this dastardly favor and I'll keep thy quiver full for a season.
Huntsman Puck: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck out there!
Wolf Pack defeated! (1/10) | Quest 'Wolfman Puck': Find and slay the orc driving them - Complete!
Quest 'Wolfman Puck': Cull the wolf packs in the wilds - Complete! | All steps done! Return to the quest giver for your reward.
The party defeated Wolves and Goblins.
Huntsman Puck: Thank you, hero! Here is your reward: +200 XP, +200 Gold, +Arrows

The party defeated Goblin Raiding Party.

Sage Veylin: Sit, traveler. What I tell thee now leaves this room with thee alone. The unrest in these lands — the wolves, the orcs, the dead that will not lie — they are not the sickness, only its fever. The true blight is an ancient wyrm, older than Larian, older than any scroll I have read. It sleeps beneath a shattered keep on the far northeastern island, and as it stirs the land darkens. Arm thyself well. Take the Sun Sword if thou hast the arm for it. Descend through the broken halls, past the lava that feeds its breath, past the bones of those who tried before. End the wyrm. Cut out its heart and bring it to the shrine — only there may it be undone. Do this, and Asoloth yet has a morning.
Sage Veylin: No worries. Come back if you change your mind.

Lardo the Bardo: Listen well, friend, for I sing of more than pretty tales these days. The goblin horde has taken the old stronghold to the north, and scarce a week passes without a caravan lost upon the road. If thou hast the steel for it, break their ranks at the source. Larian will remember the deed — and I shall make the reward worth thy while.
Lardo the Bardo: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck out there!

Roland: Canst thou help a fellow out? I was poking about those ruins to the north — the Derelict Caves — when something big and hungry came up the stairs. I dropped my Scroll of Fire on the way out. Normally I'd fetch it myself, but whatever is down there... well, it wants more firepower than I've got left. Bring it back and it's thine to keep the reward.  [ENTER]
Roland: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck!

Morvain the Grey: Those ruins are no orc warren alone, friend. I feel a colder hand working beneath the stone — a necromancer, unless my bones lie to me. I'll not approach alone, but if thou hast the fire for it, break the orcs and cut the strings of whatever pulls them. I'll see thee armed for the work.  [ENTER]
Morvain the Grey: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck!

Sage Veylin: Sit, traveler. What I tell thee now leaves this room with thee alone. The unrest in these lands — the wolves, the orcs, the dead that will not lie — they are not the sickness, only its fever. The true blight is an ancient wyrm, older than Larian, older than any scroll I have read. It sleeps beneath a shattered keep on the far northeastern island, and as it stirs the land darkens. Arm thyself well. Take the Sun Sword if thou hast the arm for it. Descend through the broken halls, past the lava that feeds its breath, past the bones of those who tried before. End the wyrm. Cut out its heart and bring it to the shrine — only there may it be undone. Do this, and Asoloth yet has a morning.
Sage Veylin: No worries. Come back if you change your mind.
The party defeated Boss: Goblin, Goblin, Goblin, Goblin, Dark Mage, Orc Shaman, Orc.

Laird the Lord: I'll be plain with thee, sellsword. There is an old temple called the Citadel of the Sun upon an island far to the east. Within it sleeps a blade forged in daylight — the Sun Sword. Find a boat, brave the deep, and bring it back. If what I fear wakes, this blade may be our only salvation.  [ENTER]
Laird the Lord: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck!
